feedback.text: The contribution of the Central European Lawyers Initiative (CELI) is provided in the attached document.

sections.title: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle

paragraphs.text: The 2024 Fitness Check (evaluation) on digital fairness identified gaps in consumer protection online.
This initiative, taking into account the existing EU digital rulebook, aims to tackle problematic practices such as:
unfair commercial practices related to dark patterns;
misleading marketing by influencers;
addictive design of digital products; and
unfair personalisation practices.
It also aims to ensure a level playing field for online traders, facilitate enforcement and introduce possible simplifications.

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: Thanks to the evolution of EU consumer law over the last 50 years, EU consumers are among the most protected in the world, both online and offline. Recent digital legislation has brought significant improvements in the area of consumer protection. However, the Fitness Check of EU consumer law on digital fairness concluded that while existing consumer protection rules remain relevant and necessary to ensure a high level of consumer protection and an effective functioning of the Digital Single Market, they are not fit to fully address the specific harmful practices and challenges consumers face online.

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: The findings of the Fitness Check highlight some shortcomings in the protection of consumers and areas of legal uncertainty. In particular, consumers are too often exposed to practices such as the design of deceptive or manipulative interfaces (dark patterns), addictive functionalities, unfair personalisation practices that exploit consumer vulnerabilities, misleading commercial practices of online influencers, as well as difficulties in managing digital contracts. The existing rules, largely based on principles, also do not provide sufficient legal certainty for businesses or authorities.

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: In recent years, the EU has adopted several new pieces of legislation significantly strengthening its digital rulebook, such as the Digital Services Act, the Digital Markets Act and the Artificial Intelligence Act, in addition to the Data Act and the Audiovisual Media Services Directive, among others. These instruments complement EU consumer legislation and reduce the risks and damage associated with specific problems online. In particular, the DSA introduced new restrictions on several unfair practices occurring on online platforms. Overall, the enforcement of consumer protection rules in the digital area in conjunction with other acts of digital legislation, which provide for rules on certain types of commercial operators (e.g. online platforms) or technologies (e.g. AI systems), is nevertheless complex and some specific gaps remain.

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: The key problems this initiative aims to tackle are:

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: the lack of digital fairness for consumers, including vulnerable consumers such as minors, which prevents consumers from making the best possible choice, which can lead to financial harm, loss of time, adverse health effects and indirect effects such as environmental costs, and

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: unclear rules for businesses and market fragmentation that generate increased costs for businesses, barriers to cross-border trade/loss of business opportunities and unfair competition, in particular from non-EU traders.

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: According to the Fitness Check, the financial damage caused to consumers as a result of problems encountered online is estimated to be at least EUR 7.9 billion per year, which does not sufficiently reflect the severity of the problem, as it does not take into account the loss of time or possible non-financial harm such as harm to mental integrity. Without EU intervention, the situation is likely to worsen given the increasing financial damage suffered by consumers over the years, the rapid growth

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: of e-commerce and technological developments.

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: In addition, the Commission’s objective of increasing the EU’s competitiveness includes focusing on simplification and measures to remove barriers in the Single Market. In the area of consumer protection, the Fitness Check highlighted the current lack of clarity and legal certainty regarding unfair commercial practices; it highlighted the lack of enforcement, existing regulatory gaps and market fragmentation as Member States regulate or plan to regulate in these areas. These problems have a negative impact on the single market and the level playing field for EU companies.

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: **Our opinion as an authorised consumer association in France and a member of the European Consumers Union:**

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: The EU has launched the trusted flaggers programme across all Member States and we look forward to it.

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: However, this programme is inherently very limited and its very recent deployment does not make it possible to measure its actual impact.

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: Among the issues not covered by this scheme are the Dark Patdes, whose variety and over-abundance lead to over-consumption, far from being of direct benefit to consumers and deeply mortiferous in terms of climate challenges. It is at this level that the main Gordian node in the EU is located: want to protect its citizens through a set of legal measures while at the same time seeking to develop an ultra-liberal policy issued from any hindrance.

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: The challenges we are currently facing cannot in any way be resolved through such a posture. While it is admirable to seek to overcome the “lack of digital fairness for [...] vulnerable consumers such as minors [...] which can lead to adverse health effects and indirect effects such as environmental costs for them”, this will in no way be compatible with the EU’s obsession to reduce the rules that affect businesses and allow them to grow their business without limits.

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: Thinking that this is just about unfair competition from companies located in countries that do not adopt these regulations invariably leads to a simple desire to deregulate. The speech is the same each time: since we cannot change others, in the name of competitiveness, we do not impose overly prescriptive rules. This “argument” is always the same, be it agricultural, digital or other products.

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: Digital fairness, while relying on a strong legislative body, can only be at this level, which will be constantly threatened and undermined by commercial interests.

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: It must include:

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: Maintaining the digital option instead of the digital obligation, a phenomenon which has been developing widely in recent years and which leads to the abolition of any human alternative (welcome desk, information centre, support community, etc.),

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: Significant efforts at the level of all the Member States to assist consumers and users in the use of digital tools,

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: The obligation to publish source codes and algorithms of commercial companies whenever their programmes directly or indirectly affect the sale of goods and/or services,

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: Moratoria on the deployment of new technologies, including their social and environmental impact, political intentionality, the nature of the actors involved in their development, and any possibility of gradual deployment. Such moratoria must include representative consumer associations.

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: Without properly analysing the impact of products such as smartphones or social networks among young consumers, and without weighing the weight of the appeal of virtual and anonymity (even if it is in fact only frontal) in the face of a society struggling to propose a common mobilising project (even though the European authorities have always sought to persuade themselves of the opposite in a reassuring way), we are now faced with the finding of a wide degree of powerlessness against digital addiction and inappropriate behaviour by the same young people, that the ad nauseam development of an allegedly protective legal body will never inhibit.

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: The 'protection' of minors from access to pornographic digital content was immediately fractured by the explosion in sales of VPN. Whatever the legal volume adopted, it will be immediately circumvented and challenged by the undertakings that hurt the barrier. And if the digital space were to be so locked that control could be fully effective, then it would lose its attractiveness, which is precisely to be able to sell it as openly as possible, be it products, services or ideas.

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: Because digital is a political project well before it is a technology.

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: He wonders what we expect from our society, for us, our children, and all their successors.

section_path: Problem the initiative aims to tackle
paragraphs.text: If we sincerely aim at digital fairness, then we must fundamentally call into question the social, economic and environmental assumptions of the EU today, and in particular the European Commission.

we deny individualism as a founding principle of society. Digital technology offers the vision of a atomised society where the collective would necessarily be ephemeral and desensitised, oriented towards the short-term enjoyment of an individual benefit. However, the very principle of society that we defend and help build through our voluntary work is a long time, training and joint reflection. While digital tools are ways to facilitate this work, they should not become the fundamental principle.

we are fighting digital pre-determinism. Digital technology is a powerful back-up tool that must leave the last word to the human. We must be extremely vigilant against the cultural hegemony that is built on the basis of medium or medium data (data aggregation principle) or through weightings which are themselves highly questionable in their buildings. These methods of constructing the standard remove the particularities and prevent them from being effectively addressed. However, through our action on the ground, we are essentially confronted with such singularities. That is the very principle of the human being.

these demands lead us to affirm the principle of digital inclusion as fundamental. In a society where access to all social and institutional services and places integrates a share of digital, which is constantly growing, no one should be left behind. We insist that digital inclusion includes possession of suitable hardware, mastery of its functioning and access to deployed software logics far from being universal contrary to the assumption of institutional discourse.
